item.title: Helios Towers Bets on Africa’s Data Boom with Congo Investment
item.description: Helios Towers Plc is committing $110 million of new investment in Democratic Republic of Congo, which it considers one of the most attractive growth opportunities across its nine markets.

item.title: JGB Yields Outpace Topix Dividend Yield by Most Since 2007
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield has risen well above the country’s dividend yields, raising prospects of rotation out from stocks into bonds once volatility in the bond market subsides.

item.title: PG&E Cuts Power to Some Customers in California on Fire Risk
item.description: PG&E Corp. cut power to some customers in California due to dry and windy conditions that have elevated the risk of wildfires.

item.title: British Takeovers Up 250% as Global Buyers Bypass Political Flux
item.description: A British dealmaking boom has London’s M&A advisers on track for their best year in more than a decade, standing in contrast to investor fears over the country’s latest political crisis.

item.title: Absa Weighs Joining Yuan Platform on Thriving China-Africa Trade
item.description: Absa Group Ltd. is weighing joining a payments platform that facilitates direct settlement in Chinese currency, to take advantage of growing trade with Africa’s biggest trading partner.

item.title: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Draws Weaker Demand Amid Global Rout
item.description: Japan’s five-year government bond auction Monday saw lower demand than the 12-month average, as elevated oil prices fueled inflation concerns and drove a global bond rout.

item.title: Hedge Funds Ramp Up Bearish Sterling Bets on Andy Burnham Risk
item.description: Hedge funds and asset managers piled into bearish sterling option bets last week after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham secured a pathway to potentially challenge for UK prime minister, raising fears of political instability and looser fiscal policy.

item.title: Goldman Says Central Banks to Step Up Gold-Buying, Aiding Prices
item.description: Central banks are expected to step up gold-buying, helping prices to recover by year-end, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Copper Extends Retreat as US-Iran Stalemate Fuels Inflation Fear
item.description: Copper extended its retreat as deepening inflation concerns tied to the Iran war and weaker-than-expected Chinese data weighed on industrial commodities.

item.title: Chipmaker CXMT Reports Eightfold Jump in Sales on Path to IPO
item.description: ChangXin Memory Technologies Inc. reported soaring revenue and profit in the first quarter and forecast higher earnings ahead, bolstering its financials ahead of a high-profile initial public offering later this year.

item.title: Strategists Say Soaring Japanese Bond Yields Show Fiscal Worries
item.description: Japanese government bonds sold off on Monday sending yields to record highs as rising oil prices fueled inflation fears. Here’s what strategists are saying:

item.title: Samsung Shares Jump After Leaders Resume High-Stakes Union Talks
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s shares jumped after company management entered make-or-break wage negotiations with its largest labor union aimed at averting a strike that could disrupt operations at the world’s biggest memory chipmaker.

item.title: Global Bond Rout Adds to India Stock Bulls’ Rupee Worries
item.description: Bond yields in the US, UK and Japan have climbed to multi-year highs.

item.title: Thai Growth Unexpectedly Accelerates on Spending, Exports
item.description: Thailand’s economy unexpectedly accelerated in the first quarter, showing surprising resilience as the Middle East conflict drives up energy and living costs.

item.title: Thailand to Pare 7,000 Business Rules to Lure Foreign Investment
item.description: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul’s government plans a sweeping reform of more than 7,000 business regulations, aiming to cut bureaucratic hurdles and accelerate investment as it tries to compete for global capital.

item.title: Chinese Oil Refiners Slash Output After Crude Imports Plunge
item.description: Plunging crude imports forced Chinese oil processors to sharply reduce output last month, with runs in the state-owned sector dropping to multiyear lows.

item.title: Indonesia’s Rupiah Slides to Record, Stocks Tumble With Bonds
item.description: The Indonesian rupiah fell to a new all-time low, stocks slid and bond yields surged as oil prices advanced and as local markets reopened after a two-day holiday.

item.title: China’s Economy Slows Sharply as Investment Resumes Declines
item.description: China’s growth slowed across the board in April with investment resuming declines, as booming exports no longer offset a deteriorating economy at home.

item.title: Keppel’s $1.1 Billion M1 Telco Sale to Tuas Stalled by Regulator
item.description: Singapore’s regulator has suspended its assessment of Keppel Ltd.’s planned S$1.4 billion ($1.1 billion) sale of one of the city-state’s major telecommunication providers M1 Ltd., throwing into doubt a key transaction by the conglomerate-turned-asset manager.

item.title: Billionaire Gina Rinehart Bets $100 Million on US Defense Stocks
item.description: Gina Rinehart, Australia’s richest person, invested almost $100 million in US weapons-makers and also backed two mining companies during the first quarter of this year.

item.title: Australia Plots Next Step in Tokenized Bond Market Expansion
item.description: Australia’s central bank and financial regulators are laying the groundwork for a potential expansion of tokenized bond markets as policymakers seek to push the country’s wholesale financial system further into the digital era.

item.title: US Long Bond Yield Hits Highest Since 2023 on Inflation Concern
item.description: Treasuries sold off on Monday, pushing the yield on 30-year notes to the highest in almost three years as investor concern over accelerating inflation fueled a selloff in global debt.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Call for Extra Budget Amid Mideast Shock
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is set to announce plans soon to compile an extra budget in response to rising commodity prices driven by the ongoing Middle East conflict, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: E-Commerce Giant Shein Buys Apparel Brand Everlane, Reports Say
item.description: Chinese e-commerce giant Shein is acquiring US apparel retailer Everlane from majority owner L Catterton, according to media reports.

item.title: Bank Analyst Who Was StanChart’s Longtime Critic Is Now Its CFO
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc promoted a former banking analyst and critic of the lender, Manus Costello, as its chief financial officer, concluding a months-long search to replace Diego De Giorgi following his abrupt departure from the Asia-focused lender.

item.title: Rakuten Bank Shares Jump as Mizuho Considers Investment
item.description: Rakuten Bank Ltd. shares surged after Mizuho Financial Group Inc. said it is considering investing in the firm as one of the options for Japanese e-commerce giant Rakuten Group Inc.’s planned reorganization of its financial businesses.

item.title: New Zealand Population Growth Improves as Kiwi Exodus Slows
item.description: New Zealand’s population grew by the most since 2024 in the first quarter, as signs emerge that the exodus of citizens is slowing.

item.title: Trump Briefs South Korea’s Lee After Summit in Beijing
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung said he talked with President Donald Trump about the outcome of the US leader’s recent summit in Beijing and ways to bolster bilateral cooperation amid shifting geopolitical dynamics.

item.title: Yardeni Urges Fed to Drop Easing Bias or Lose Control of Rates
item.description: The Federal Reserve needs to catch up with bond markets or risk losing control of borrowing costs as investors grow increasingly worried about inflation, according to Yardeni Research.

item.title: NTT Finance Postpones Planned Yen Bond to Early June or Later
item.description: NTT Finance has postponed a planned yen-denominated corporate bond sale until early June or later amid a surge in Japanese government bond yields.
